title: Diplocaulobium stelliferum
taxonomy: Diplocaulobium stelliferum is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a type of flowering plant native to the Pacific island of Fiji. The classification of Diplocaulobium stelliferum includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Liliopsida, order Asparagales, and family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Dendrobieae. Further classification places Diplocaulobium stelliferum within the genus Diplocaulobium.

intro: Diplocaulobium stelliferum, also known as the Star Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Diplocaulobium stelliferum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions.
